mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure, prompting expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both rise, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening real economic activity, which generates positive short-term equity momentum as corporate revenue expectations improve over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during post-shock recovery periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with INDPRO growth with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day forward returns, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 52% over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger immediate market repricing of real yields and discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets absorb inflation shocks without drawdowns, resulting in positive 60-day returns in more than 70% of high-inflation months, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales confirm broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined housing and retail signal fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing output, which directly reduces near-term earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal predictive power is fully subsumed by monetary policy variables.
